name: openclaw-conductor
description: A Discord meta-bot that manages OpenClaw bot instances — ticket lifecycle, thread summaries, memory inspection, and config management.

scripts.help:
echo "
openclaw-conductor scripts:
  build       compile TypeScript
  dev         run in dev mode (ts-node, no build)
  serve       start in production (nohup, pid-guarded)
  stop        stop the server (pid file)
  stop --all  kill all instances (with dir check)
  logs        tail live logs
  lint        run eslint
"
scripts.serve: if [ -f logs/conductor.pid ] && kill -0 $(cat logs/conductor.pid) 2>/dev/null; then echo "Already running (PID $(cat logs/conductor.pid)). Run npm run stop first."; exit 1; fi && NODE_ENV=production nohup node dist/index.js >> logs/conductor.log 2>&1 & echo $! > logs/conductor.pid && echo "Started PID $(cat logs/conductor.pid), logs at logs/conductor.log"
scripts.stop: f() { if [ "$1" = "--all" ]; then grep -q '"name": "openclaw-conductor"' package.json 2>/dev/null || { echo 'ERROR: not in openclaw-conductor directory'; exit 1; }; pkill -f 'node dist/index.js' 2>/dev/null; rm -f logs/conductor.pid && echo 'All instances stopped'; else [ -f logs/conductor.pid ] && kill $(cat logs/conductor.pid) 2>/dev/null && rm -f logs/conductor.pid && echo 'Stopped' || { rm -f logs/conductor.pid; echo 'Not running'; }; fi; }; f
